details.text: Derivative: تَحَاوُرَ from تَحَاوَرَ and is from the meaning of حِوَارٌ which means a dialogue, a back-and-forth discussion. The difference between تَحَاوُر and حِوَار is that تَحَاوُر indicates to a more mutual dialogue and exchange, whilst حِوَار implies a more direct and intense dialogue.
Derivative: تَشْتَكِي from اِشْتَكَى. It means to complain like شَكَى however, اِشْتَكَى is stronger due to the additional letters and hence means to complain a lot or to complain stronger.

Derivative: انْشُزُوا from نَشَزَ – يَنْشُزُ – نُشُوز means to rise up from one’s place and can apply abruptness in rising up. It is also used to describe recalcitrance or rebellious disobedience from the husband or wife towards the other, as if one is rising up against the other i.e., in terms of their rights.

- أَيْمَانَهُمْ “Their oaths” refers to their testimony of belief. Here, they take the fact that they have testified to Islam as a shield (جُنَّةً) to afford them protection and give them room to engage in nefarious and undermining activities.
- عَلَىٰ شَيْءٍ “Upon something” means to have some basis or to stand on some ground.

Derivative: اسْتَحْوَذَ – يَسْتَحْوِذُ – اِسْتِحْواذَا. حَاذَ means to spur on and steer an animal with great control. اسْتَحْوَذَ means to gain complete control over something of somebody, to overwhelm and gain mastery over something or someone.

Derivative: مُنْكَرًا comes from the word نَكِرَ – يَنْكُرُ which means not know and أَنْكَرَ means to deny, like a denial of knowledge of something. It is the opposite of مَعْروف (Ma’ruf) which is that which is easily recognised and known. مُنْكَرًا here means that it is alien or unfamiliar to anyone with a sense of decency i.e., from that it is unacceptable, disapproved of and associated with evil.
Derivative: زُورًا is a false accusation that is designed to cause hurt to somebody. It also used to refer to false testimony that will cause harm to somebody in a trial.

Derivative: يُظَاهِرُونَ from ظَاهَرَ – ظِهَار. يُظَاهِرُونَ means that they perpetrated ظِهَار and it refers to a pre-Islamic custom where the husband would say to his wife: “You are like my mother or like my mother’s back” which means that “I no longer regard you as my wife any longer and you are like my mother and no longer Halal for me i.e., in terms of intimacy” and it implies divorce.
Derivative: مُتَتَابِعَيْنِ means two consecutive months which implies that no break can be taken and that the days of the two months are consecutive.
Derivative: مُهِينٌ means humiliating and comes from إِهانَة, هُون and هَوْن which carry the meanings of degradation and humiliation.
Derivative: كُبِتُوا means to be humiliated and then destroyed.
Derivative: يُحَادُّونَ from حَادَّ – يُحَادُّ means to be in stringent opposition; an iron (from حَدِيد), unbending attitude and position against something or someone.
Derivative: يُنَبِّئُ from نَبَّأَ (Nabba’a) means to thoroughly inform. أَنْبَأَ (Anba’a) means to inform whilst نَبَّأَ (Nabba’a) means to thoroughly inform.
Derivative: أَحْصَا literally means to count using pebbles (حَصَاة). It means to count and enumerate in a precise and complete manner.

Derivative: نَجْوَىٰ means to escape from the larger group, to be by themselves and to hold a meeting in private. It refers to a secret conversation, conferring or a meeting that a small group engages in. It differs from Shura (consultation) in terms of it not being open and its subject area being secret or confidential. Such secret conversations or meetings hold the potential for undermining, plotting and conspiring to take place.
- الْإِثْمِ (Sin) refers to an individual spiritual harm whilst الْعُدْوَانِ (hostility and animosity against others) from عَدَا - يَعْدُو refers to a societal harm.
